text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Gerald T. Holloway
MRN: [REDACTED]
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]
ATTENDING SURGEON: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED]
CLINICAL SERVICE: Colorectal Surgery
PROCEDURE DATE: [REDACTED]
DATE RECEIVED: [REDACTED]
DATE REPORTED: [REDACTED]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CLINICAL INFORMATION / INDICATION:
Patient is a 58-year-old male with newly diagnosed stage IVA colorectal adenocarcinoma. Preoperative colonoscopic biopsy confirmed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon. Preoperative staging CT demonstrated a 4.8 cm sigmoid mass with 4 synchronous hepatic metastases (largest 3.1 cm, segment VI). Preoperative molecular testing: KRAS G12D mutation, NRAS wild-type, BRAF V600E wild-type, MSS by IHC. Procedure performed: laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy with primary anastomosis for resection of the primary tumor prior to initiation of systemic therapy for metastatic disease. Liver metastases not addressed at this surgery.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
  A. Sigmoid colon resection (laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy with mesorectal/mesocolic envelope, primary anastomosis performed)
  B. Apical/vascular margin lymph node (separately submitted by surgeon)
  C. Donut rings (proximal and distal anastomotic donuts, submitted separately intraoperatively -- reported as negative by frozen section; permanent sections confirm)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FINAL PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:

A. SIGMOID COLON RESECTION:
   INVASIVE MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED ADENOCARCINOMA, CONVENTIONAL TYPE
   - Histologic type: Adenocarcinoma, NOS (conventional glandular)
   - Histologic grade: G2 (moderately differentiated)
   - Tumor size: 4.8 x 3.9 x 3.2 cm
   - Primary tumor (pT): pT3 -- tumor invades through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal adipose tissue; visceral peritoneum is NOT penetrated (no serosal surface involvement)
   - Regional lymph nodes (pN): pN2a -- 3 of 18 regional lymph nodes positive for metastatic adenocarcinoma
     * Lymph nodes examined: 18
     * Lymph nodes positive: 3
     * Largest positive lymph node: 1.1 cm
     * Extranodal extension: ABSENT
   - Distant metastasis (pM): pM1a (synchronous hepatic metastases confirmed by imaging; not resected at this procedure)
   - Overall pathologic stage: pT3 pN2a pM1a -- Stage IVA (AJCC 8th edition)

   MARGINS:
   - Proximal resection margin: NEGATIVE for malignancy (distance from tumor: 8.0 cm)
   - Distal resection margin: NEGATIVE for malignancy (distance from tumor: 6.0 cm)
   - Radial (circumferential resection) margin (CRM): NEGATIVE for malignancy (closest distance from tumor to inked radial margin: 4 mm)
   - Overall resection status: R0 (complete resection, all margins negative)

   LYMPHOVASCULAR INVASION: PRESENT, extensive
      -- Multiple foci of tumor cells within lymphovascular spaces identified in pericolorectal adipose tissue, predominantly lymphatic
   PERINEURAL INVASION: PRESENT
      -- Identified at the deep invasive front; large-caliber perineural sheaths involved
   TUMOR DEPOSITS: 1 (one discrete tumor deposit identified in pericolorectal adipose tissue, 0.6 cm, without identifiable residual lymph node architecture -- consistent with discontinuous tumor spread/venous invasion)
   TUMOR BUDDING: HIGH (Bd3) -- greater than 10 buds per 0.785 mm2 field at invasive front by international consensus scoring
   MUCINOUS COMPONENT: Absent (less than 50% mucinous; focal dirty intraluminal necrosis present)
   SIGNET RING CELL COMPONENT: Absent
   BACKGROUND COLON: No adenomatous polyps identified. Mild diverticular change in adjacent sigmoid mucosa. No evidence of colitis or dysplasia in background mucosa.
   SEROSA: Intact; no tumor on serosal surface (tumor does not penetrate visceral peritoneum)

B. APICAL/VASCULAR MARGIN LYMPH NODE:
   ONE LYMPH NODE, NEGATIVE FOR METASTATIC CARCINOMA (0/1)

C. DONUT RINGS (PROXIMAL AND DISTAL):
   NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY, BOTH RINGS
   -- Mucosa with no significant abnormality; surgical margin sites confirmed negative

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SYNOPTIC PATHOLOGY SUMMARY (CAP PROTOCOL -- COLORECTAL CARCINOMA):

Procedure: Sigmoid colectomy
Specimen Integrity: Intact
Tumor Site: Sigmoid colon
Tumor Size: Greatest dimension 4.8 cm
Histologic Type: Adenocarcinoma, NOS
Histologic Grade: G2, moderately differentiated
Microscopic Tumor Extension: Tumor invades through muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues
Treatment Effect: Not applicable (no neoadjuvant therapy)
Margins: All margins negative (R0)
  - Proximal margin: Negative (8.0 cm)
  - Distal margin: Negative (6.0 cm)
  - Circumferential/Radial margin: Negative (4 mm)
Lymphovascular Invasion: Present, extensive
Perineural Invasion: Present
Microsatellite Instability/Mismatch Repair Status: See Ancillary Studies -- MSS by IHC (MLH1 retained, MSH2 retained, MSH6 retained, PMS2 retained)
Tumor Deposits: 1
Tumor Budding: Bd3 (high)
Regional Lymph Nodes:
  - Total examined: 18 (includes 1 separately submitted apical node)
  - Total positive: 3
  - Total negative: 15
  - Extranodal extension: Not identified
pTNM Stage: pT3 pN2a pM1a -- Stage IVA

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:

1. IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY -- MISMATCH REPAIR PROTEIN EXPRESSION (performed on resection specimen, representative tumor blocks):
   MLH1: RETAINED nuclear expression (positive)
   MSH2: RETAINED nuclear expression (positive)
   MSH6: RETAINED nuclear expression (positive)
   PMS2: RETAINED nuclear expression (positive)
   INTERPRETATION: Intact mismatch repair protein expression in all 4 proteins. Findings are consistent with a MICROSATELLITE-STABLE (MSS) tumor. No evidence of mismatch repair deficiency.
   Internal control: adequate (normal colonic epithelium shows appropriate nuclear staining)

2. IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY -- TUMOR CHARACTERIZATION (performed on representative tumor blocks):
   CK20: POSITIVE (strong, diffuse cytoplasmic and membranous)
   CDX2: POSITIVE (strong, diffuse nuclear)
   CK7: NEGATIVE
   INTERPRETATION: Immunophenotype is consistent with colorectal primary adenocarcinoma. (Note: additional IHC for systemic/trial correlation may be sent per oncology request.)

3. KRAS SEQUENCING (reflex, hotspot panel; performed on representative FFPE tumor section from this resection specimen for independent confirmation on surgical specimen):
   RESULT: KRAS p.G12D (c.35G>A) -- MUTATION DETECTED
   Method: PCR-based hotspot mutation analysis, codons 12 and 13
   NOTE: Confirmatory; consistent with prior colonoscopic biopsy result. Full extended RAS testing (NRAS exons 2-4, KRAS exons 3-4) and BRAF V600E are documented from prior biopsy as wild-type. Repeat extended panel not performed on this specimen per institutional protocol when prior adequate molecular testing is documented; clinical team may request if needed for systemic therapy planning.

4. HER2 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY: PENDING
   (Ordered by oncology team for therapeutic decision-making. Will be reported as addendum.)

5. ADDITIONAL MOLECULAR TESTING: Comprehensive tumor NGS (500-gene panel) previously performed on colonoscopic biopsy specimen; results on file. Repeat NGS on surgical specimen deferred per clinical team discussion; no additional tissue sent for NGS at this time.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:

SPECIMEN A -- SIGMOID COLON RESECTION:
Received fresh, labeled "sigmoid colon," is a segment of large bowel measuring 28.0 cm in length and up to 4.5 cm in luminal diameter in areas of uninvolved bowel. The specimen is received with an attached mesentery and mesorectal/mesocolic envelope. The serosal surface is smooth and glistening over the majority of the specimen without adhesions or obvious tumor involvement. The proximal and distal surgical resection margins are identified and inked separately (proximal -- blue; distal -- black). The external surface is inked circumferentially with yellow ink for radial margin assessment.

Serial sectioning reveals a firm, tan-white, exophytic and partially ulcerated circumferential mass measuring 4.8 x 3.9 x 3.2 cm located in the sigmoid colon, centered 8.0 cm from the proximal margin and 6.0 cm from the distal margin. The tumor has a pale tan-white to focally hemorrhagic cut surface with irregular borders. The mass occupies approximately 60% of the luminal circumference at its maximal extent and narrows but does not completely obstruct the lumen. The overlying mucosa at the tumor site is ulcerated centrally with raised, heaped-up, firm edges. On cross section, tumor invasion is seen through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal adipose tissue. The serosal/peritoneal surface overlying the mass is intact and does not appear grossly involved by tumor, with an estimated closest approach of tumor to the radial inked margin of approximately 4 mm at the deepest point of invasion. No gross perforation or serosal tumor nodules are identified.

The remaining colonic mucosa (proximal and distal to the mass) is tan-pink, velvety, and grossly unremarkable without identifiable polyps or other masses. Pericolorectal adipose tissue is submitted in its entirety for lymph node sampling. Gross dissection of the mesorectal/mesocolic fat yields multiple lymph node candidates, submitted in their entirety per standard protocol.

Representative sections submitted:
- A1-A4: Tumor in full thickness sections (including relationship to deepest radial margin, serosal surface, and transition to uninvolved mucosa)
- A5: Additional sections of tumor with pericolorectal fat (deepest invasion front)
- A6: Proximal resection margin (en face)
- A7: Distal resection margin (en face)
- A8: Representative uninvolved sigmoid mucosa
- A9-A18: All lymph node candidates from pericolorectal fat (entirety submitted, 17 candidate nodes grossly identified -- processed to yield 17 nodes for histologic examination)

SPECIMEN B -- APICAL/VASCULAR MARGIN LYMPH NODE:
Received in formalin, labeled "apical lymph node," is a tan-pink ovoid lymph node measuring 1.4 x 0.9 x 0.6 cm. Serial sectioning reveals tan-white homogeneous cut surface without grossly identifiable tumor. Entirely submitted in one cassette.
- B1: Apical lymph node, entirety

SPECIMEN C -- DONUT RINGS (PROXIMAL AND DISTAL ANASTOMOTIC DONUTS):
Received in separate labeled containers, both submitted during the intraoperative period for frozen section assessment and reported as negative. Each ring is a circumferential fragment of colorectal wall measuring approximately 0.5-0.8 cm in diameter and 0.3-0.5 cm in thickness. Grossly unremarkable.
- C1: Proximal donut ring, permanent sections (post-frozen)
- C2: Distal donut ring, permanent sections (post-frozen)

Total cassettes submitted: 18

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Sections of the primary tumor (specimen A) show an invasive adenocarcinoma with moderately differentiated (G2) glandular architecture. The tumor is composed of irregular, angulated neoplastic glands with stratified nuclei exhibiting moderate nuclear pleomorphism, prominent nucleoli, and numerous mitotic figures. Focal areas of intraluminal "dirty necrosis" (luminal necrosis with nuclear debris) are noted, a characteristic feature of conventional colorectal adenocarcinoma. There is no mucinous component (less than 10% extracellular mucin). No signet ring cells are identified.

At the invasive front, the tumor extends through the full thickness of the muscularis propria into the pericolorectal adipose tissue as irregular jagged tongues of neoplastic glands. The visceral peritoneum/serosal surface is examined on multiple levels and does NOT show tumor cells at the peritoneal surface; the closest approach of tumor to the serosal surface is estimated at greater than 1 mm on histologic sections. The circumferential (radial) inked margin is clear of tumor by 4 mm at the point of deepest invasion.

Tumor budding at the invasive front is prominent and scored as Bd3 (high-grade budding, greater than 10 tumor buds per standardized high-power field), consistent with increased invasive and metastatic potential.

Extensive lymphovascular invasion is identified: multiple tumor cell clusters are present within thin-walled endothelium-lined spaces in the pericolorectal adipose tissue. Immunohistochemistry with D2-40 (podoplanin) highlights intralymphatic tumor emboli in at least 5 separate foci.

Perineural invasion is present: tumor cells are identified surrounding and tracking along large-caliber nerve fibers within pericolorectal fat.

One pericolorectal tumor deposit is identified as a discrete irregular nodule of adenocarcinoma in the pericolorectal adipose tissue measuring 0.6 cm in greatest dimension, without residual lymph node architecture or vascular wall, consistent with tumor deposit (TD category per AJCC 8th edition).

Three of the 18 regional lymph nodes examined (including 17 from specimen A and 1 apical node from specimen B) contain metastatic adenocarcinoma. The three positive nodes measure 1.1 cm, 0.8 cm, and 0.5 cm in greatest dimension; all show partial to near-complete replacement by metastatic carcinoma. Extranodal extension is NOT identified in any of the involved nodes. The remaining 15 lymph nodes are negative for metastatic carcinoma, showing reactive changes only.

The proximal and distal surgical margins (en face sections, A6 and A7) and both anastomotic donut rings (C1 and C2) are negative for malignancy, confirmed on permanent sections.

Background sigmoid and uninvolved colonic mucosa shows mild reactive cryptitis without dysplasia. No adenomatous polyps, serrated polyps, or additional primary neoplasms are identified. Mild diverticular change with mucosal herniation is noted in the adjacent sigmoid. No evidence of inflammatory bowel disease.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMPRESSION / SUMMARY:

Sigmoid colon: Moderately differentiated (G2) invasive adenocarcinoma, conventional type, pT3 pN2a pM1a (Stage IVA, AJCC 8th edition). R0 resection with all margins negative. Extensive lymphovascular invasion and perineural invasion present. High-grade tumor budding (Bd3). One tumor deposit. Intact mismatch repair protein expression (MSS). KRAS G12D mutation confirmed on this resection specimen. Findings are consistent with the known metastatic colorectal carcinoma in this clinical context.

ADDENDUM (PENDING):
HER2 IHC result to follow as separate addendum report when staining is complete.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Pathologist signature: [REDACTED], MD
Board-Certified Anatomic and Clinical Pathologist
Date finalized: [REDACTED]

Electronic verification and signature on file.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
